skill.name: fda-database
skill.description: Query the openFDA API to retrieve FDA regulatory datasets (drugs, devices, adverse events, recalls, submissions, UNII) when you need programmatic safety/regulatory evidence for analysis or research.
skill.summary: Python interface to query openFDA API for drug safety, device regulation, food recalls, and substance data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After evaluation, all 2691 static findings are false positives. C2 keywords, SAM database, and browser credential hits in fda_cache files are medical terminology in cached FDA API responses. Weak crypto alerts come from MD5 cache key hashing (non-security use). Shell backtick patterns in markdown files are code documentation examples. Network calls target openFDA API only. The skill is a legitimate regulatory data query tool with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: MD5 hash used for cache key generation
low_findings.description: FDAQuery uses MD5 hashing for cache key generation. This is a non-security use (cache indexing only) but triggered the weak cryptographic algorithm detector. No security impact.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in cached API response files
low_findings.description: fda_cache JSON files contain hardcoded URLs from the openFDA API. These are cached responses from legitimate API calls to api.fda.gov.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: References/*.md files contain shell backtick code examples showing curl commands for openFDA API. These are documentation only, not executed code.

low_findings.title: C2 keywords detected in cached FDA response data
low_findings.description: Medical terminology in cached FDA API responses (drug names, device classifications) triggered C2 keyword pattern matches. These are legitimate FDA data terms, not command-and-control indicators.

content.user_title: Query FDA Databases for Drug Safety and Regulatory Research
content.value_statement: Regulatory researchers need access to FDA adverse event reports, device clearances, and recall data but navigating the openFDA API requires complex query syntax. This skill provides a unified Python interface with built-in caching, rate limiting, and convenience methods for querying all major FDA datasets (drugs, devices, foods, substances) through simple function calls.
actual_capabilities: Query drug adverse events, labels, NDC directory, and recalls by drug name or manufacturer
Retrieve medical device 510(k) clearances, PMA approvals, classification, UDI, and adverse events
Search food and veterinary adverse events and recalls by product, allergen, or species
Look up substance information by UNII code, CAS number, or chemical name
Aggregate and count results by any FDA data field with exact phrase matching
Retrieve all matching results with automatic pagination and configurable caching
limitations: Requires internet access to query the openFDA public API (no offline mode)
Subject to openFDA rate limits; optional API key recommended for higher quotas
API response structure varies by domain and endpoint; results require field-specific parsing
Cached data may become stale; cache TTL defaults to 1 hour

use_cases.title: Pharmacovigilance Safety Profile
use_cases.description: A drug safety analyst needs to compile adverse event counts, common reactions, and serious event rates for a specific drug across multiple FDA databases.
use_cases.target_user: Pharmacovigilance specialist or drug safety researcher

use_cases.title: Medical Device Regulatory Clearance Research
use_cases.description: A medical device consultant needs to verify 510(k) clearance history, product classification, and adverse event reports for a specific device type before market entry.

use_cases.title: Food Recall Monitoring and Enforcement Tracking
use_cases.description: A public health researcher monitors recent food recalls related to specific allergens and tracks enforcement classification, status, and initiation dates.
use_cases.target_user: Public health analyst or food safety researcher

prompt_templates.title: Basic Drug Adverse Event Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the openFDA API for adverse event reports related to [drug_name]. Show the total report count, the top 10 most common adverse reactions, and the number of serious events.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial drug safety screening for a researcher new to FDA data

prompt_templates.title: Medical Device 510(k) and Classification Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the openFDA device database for [product_code_or_name]. Retrieve the device classification, recent 510(k) clearances, and summarize any adverse event trends for this device type.
prompt_templates.scenario: Regulatory clearance research for a medical device consultant

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Database Drug Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the safety profiles of [drug_A] and [drug_B] using openFDA data. For each drug, retrieve total adverse events, serious event counts, serious event percentage, and check for recent recalls. Present the results as a comparison table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Competitive drug safety analysis for a pharmaceutical researcher

prompt_templates.title: Substance Identity Resolution and Chemical Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the substance [substance_name] in the openFDA substance database. Return the UNII code, CAS numbers, molecular formula, molecular weight, and substance class. If available, also list related substances.
prompt_templates.scenario: Chemical identity verification for a regulatory submission specialist

output_examples.input: Query openFDA for aspirin drug safety profile: total adverse events, top reactions, serious events, and recent recalls.
output_examples.output:
Drug Safety Profile: aspirin
- Total Adverse Event Reports: 523,441
- Top Reactions: 1. Dyspnea (45,231), 2. Urticaria (38,912), 3. Chest Pain (22,104), 4. Nausea (18,776), 5. Headache (15,443)
- Serious Events: 89,045 (17.0%)
- Recent Recalls: 2 found (Class II - labeling error, Class III - packaging defect)

output_examples.input: Query openFDA for 510(k) clearances related to pacemakers and summarize the recent trends.
output_examples.output:
Device: Pacemaker (Product Code: DQY)
- Classification: Class II, 21 CFR 870.3610
- Total Adverse Events: 12,847 reports
- Recent 510(k) Clearances (last 3): K241234 (Medtronic, 2024-06), K240567 (Boston Scientific, 2024-03), K239876 (Abbott, 2023-11)
- Event Types: Malfunction (58%), Injury (32%), Death (10%)

best_practices: Set the FDA_API_KEY environment variable for higher API rate limits and more reliable batch queries
Enable caching with an appropriate TTL to reduce redundant API calls when running repeated queries on the same dataset
Always check the meta.results.total field and handle empty result sets gracefully with fallback logic
anti_patterns: Do not use wildcard searches on unindexed fields; use exact phrase matching (.exact) for aggregation fields to avoid performance degradation
Do not query all results with large limits in a single call; use the query_all method with reasonable max_results to manage API quota
Do not ignore the API rate limit; always handle 429 responses with backoff and retry logic

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, the openFDA API supports unauthenticated access with lower daily quotas. Setting an API key via the FDA_API_KEY environment variable increases your daily request limit.

faq.question: What FDA data categories does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports drugs (events, labels, NDC, recalls), devices (events, 510k, PMA, classification, UDI), foods (events, recalls), animal/veterinary (events), and substances (UNII, chemical lookup).

faq.question: How does caching work in this skill?
faq.answer: API responses are cached as JSON files in the fda_cache directory. Cache keys are generated from the request URL and parameters. The default time-to-live is 3600 seconds (one hour).

faq.question: What are the openFDA API rate limits?
faq.answer: Unauthenticated users have a lower daily quota. With an API key, the limit increases significantly. The FDAQuery class implements rate limiting with a default of 240 requests per minute.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for real-time safety monitoring?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for research and analysis, not real-time monitoring. Cached data may be up to one hour old, and the openFDA API itself has latency. For critical safety decisions, verify with official FDA sources.

faq.question: Does this skill write to my filesystem?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill writes cached API responses to a local fda_cache directory. This improves performance by avoiding redundant API calls. You can disable caching or change the cache location.
